creator: wolfpythonlondon@gmail.com
text:
On 18 December 2011 12:03, wolf london <wolfpythonlondon@gmail.com> wrote:
> On 14 December 2011 19:59, Ryan Steinmetz <zi@freebsd.org> wrote:
>> This seems to build fine for me and does not appear to be failing on the
>> FreeBSD build cluster.
>>
>> - Are your ports (tree/installed ports) up to date?
>>
> Yes, it's updated :-)
>> - Do you have anything in /etc/make.conf?
>>
> Yes. Sorry for this PR. It's my fault. I change the CFLAGS ,which
> causes =C2=A0GCC cannot find the freetype header(s) correctly .
> After using the default CFALAGS, it builds fine.
>
Hum, I think I'm wrong again .
> # cat /etc/make.conf
> CFLAGS=3D -O2 -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe
> CXXFLAGS+=3D -fconserve-space
> FORCE_MAKE_JOBS=3Dyes
> MAKE_JOBS_NUMBER=3D4
>
>
> # added by use.perl 2011-11-16 09:50:36
> PERL_VERSION=3D5.10.1
>
>> - Are you using any options for elvis that differ from the defaults?
>>
>> - Is there any additional information you can provide that would help?
>>
yeah , here is the details

the build log and /etc/make.conf :
https://gist.github.com/1492427

As you can see, there is something wrong with the *flags* GCC uses. It
doesn't contain *-I/usr/local/include/freetype2* anymore.
>>
>> http://people.freebsd.org/~zi/elvis-2.2.0_3.log
>>
>
> You can close this PR now . Sorry for misreport .
>> -r
>>
